review_notes: The functions accurately reflect the requirement to deny all network communications by default and allow traffic by exception, which is implemented through NetworkPolicies in Kubernetes. The naming conventions are followed correctly, and the functions are descriptive and specific, clearly indicating the pass/fail condition. These functions map directly to Kubernetes security controls and are consistent with similar checks.
title: 3_13_6: 3.13.6 Deny network communications traffic by default and allow network communications traffic by exception (i.e., deny all, permit by exception)
step1_reasoning: The control requires denying all network communications by default and allowing traffic only by exception. In Kubernetes, this is achieved using NetworkPolicies. By setting a default deny policy for both ingress and egress traffic, we ensure that no traffic is allowed unless explicitly permitted by additional NetworkPolicies.
